posts.name: Jeremy Holmes
posts.cooked: This is the first bottling off the McKinley home block in Angaston. It is immediately expressive, with notes of spiced blood plum, menthol, green bean and black pepper. It is full and layered, with perfectly ripe and supportive tannins. There’s saline minerality and a finish of good authority and persistence.

posts.name: Kent Comley
posts.cooked: Sami-Odi now cult status. Vintage wines now north of $250 at auction. I am having my supply cleaned out as the word spreads.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked:
Sami-Odi now cult status. Vintage wines now north of $250 at auction. I am having my supply cleaned out as the word spreads.

You’re going to end up having to buy them in Alsace, like me

posts.name: Kent Comley
posts.cooked:
Sami-Odi now cult status. Vintage wines now north of $250 at auction. I am having my supply cleaned out as the word spreads.

You’re going to end up having to buy them in Alsace, like me

Any excuse to have a day or two off! Alsace does sound rather appealing.

posts.name: Jeremy Holmes
posts.cooked: I’ve had some wonderful and cheap bottles of Burgundy in Alsace over the years.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked:
I’ve had some wonderful and cheap bottles of Burgundy in Alsace over the years.

Fraser has an importer there, it’s where I buy the wines.

I had some shipped to the UK as a student, that was how I learnt what “Import VAT” was.

posts.name: Shay Aldriedge
posts.cooked:
This is the first bottling off the McKinley home block in Angaston. It is immediately expressive, with notes of spiced blood plum, menthol, green bean and black pepper. It is full and layered, with perfectly ripe and supportive tannins. There’s saline minerality and a finish of good authority and persistence.

Thank you for the note. I’ve been a big fan and loyal buyer off the list for five years now. The shipping from Australia to the US is brutal, but I think that speaks to Fraser’s wines. No brainer worth it for me.

Excited to try this new offering.

posts.name: Steve Anderson
posts.cooked:
This is the first bottling off the McKinley home block in Angaston. It is immediately expressive, with notes of spiced blood plum, menthol, green bean and black pepper. It is full and layered, with perfectly ripe and supportive tannins. There’s saline minerality and a finish of good authority and persistence.

Thank you for the note. I’ve been a big fan and loyal buyer off the list for five years now. The shipping from Australia to the US is brutal, but I think that speaks to Fraser’s wines. No brainer worth it for me.

Excited to try this new offering.

Jeremy - thank you for the preview on the ‘Our Hill’!

Shay - My fifth vintage as well. Haven’t opened many, but have truly enjoyed those that I have. Even with the average $25/bottle shipping/fees on a case, it still ends up much cheaper than U.S. retail, when you are able to even find a bottle.

Cheers!

posts.name: Shay Aldriedge
posts.cooked:

Cheers!

I haven’t opened many either, but every time I have, nothing but zero regrets, aside from wishing I could buy more than one case from them direct!
